data.title: Biggest stock movers Tuesday: ZIM, CMPS, DHR, and more
data.description:
Stock futures dipped Tuesday morning amid a holiday-shortened trading week, spotlighting key economic data and corporate earnings ahead.

Here are some of Tuesday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • ZIM Integrated Shipping (ZIM) +36% - Shares jumped after Hapag-Lloyd (HPGLY) agreed to acquire the shipper for $35 per share in cash, a 58% premium that values the company at about $4.2B. The deal still needs approval from regulators, shareholders, and the government of Israel, which holds a strategic “golden share” in ZIM. To help secure approval, the buyer included FIMI Opportunity Funds in the transaction.
  • COMPASS Pathways (CMPS) +20% - Shares surged after the company announced it successfully met the primary endpoint in its second Phase 3 trial of COMP360 psilocybin for treatment-resistant depression, strengthening the clinical case for the therapy and boosting expectations around a potential regulatory pathway and commercialization prospects.
  • Fiserv (FISV) +4% - Shares rose following a report that activist Jana Partners has built a stake and is pushing for steps to lift the stock, including accelerating growth in the core banking franchise and reviewing non-strategic assets. The investor is said to support the firm’s “One Fiserv” strategy and CEO Mike Lyons. Jana is not expected to seek a split of the payments and fintech businesses. The size of the stake and any board ambitions remain unclear.

Biggest stock losers

  • Ocular Therapeutix (OCUL) -27% - Shares plunged even after reporting positive topline results from its SOL-1 Phase 3 superiority trial of AXPAXLI for wet AMD, as investors appeared to focus on uncertainty around commercialization timing, durability of benefit, and next regulatory steps despite the study meeting its primary endpoint.
  • Danaher (DHR) -7% - Shares dipped amid reports it is nearing a ~$10B deal to acquire medical technology firm Masimo (MASI), a price implying a significant premium to its roughly $7B market value, while Masimo (MASI) shares jumped 34% on the news. The potential acquisition will be Danaher’s largest since buying Cytiva. It would expand its life sciences and diagnostics footprint, with Masimo known for pulse oximetry devices and its ongoing patent dispute with Apple over the Apple Watch.
  • Wheaton Precious Metals (WPM) -3% - Shares slipped after the company agreed to acquire BHP’s (BHP) 33.75% silver stream at the Antamina mine for $4.3B in cash, lifting its total share to 67.5%. The deal, effective April 2026, is expected to add ~6 Moz of annual silver output in the first five years, raise 2026 production by ~11%, and expand reserves. The deal will be funded through cash, debt, and credit facilities, leaving net debt near $2.4B at closing.

More on related stocks:

  • ZIM Integrated: Hapag-Lloyd Offer Makes Sense For Everyone, Will It Go Through?
  • Ocular Therapeutix: Considering Their Phase 3 'Superiority' Trial's Prospect
  • ZIM Integrated Shipping: Why The Buyout Matters Most - Initiating Strong Buy
  • Hapag-Lloyd to buy ZIM Integrated Shipping in $4.2B deal
  • FDA push to fast-track Compass Pathways’ psychedelic treatment rejected by Trump officials, STAT News reports

data.title: Franklin Electric misses top-line and bottom-line estimates; initiates FY26 outlook
data.description:
• Franklin Electric press release (FELE): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.87 misses by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $506.9M (+4.4% Y/Y) misses by $9.35M.
  • Net sales increased across all three segments; 4% in Water Systems, 9% in Energy Systems, and 3% in Distribution
  • Operating income was $51.6 million, an increase of 20% to the prior year, with operating margin of 10.2%.

  • Cash Flow

    The Company ended 2025 with a cash balance of $99.7 million, a decrease of $120.9 million compared to the end of 2024. Net cash flows from operating activities for 2025 were $238.9 million versus $261.4 million in the same period in 2024.

    2026 Guidance

    The Company expects its guidance for full year 2026 sales to be in the range of $2.17 billion to $2.24 billion vs. $2.24B consensus and full year 2026 Adjusted Diluted EPS to be in the range of $4.40 to $4.60 vs. $4.73 consensus.

data.title: Compass gains as psychedelic therapy succeeds in late-stage trial for depression
data.description:
Compass Pathways (CMPS) added ~20% in the premarket on Tuesday after the U.K.-based biotech announced that its psychedelic therapy COMP360 reached the primary endpoint in a late-stage trial for patients with depression.

According to the company, COMP006, the second of two pivotal Phase 3 trials for COMP360, reached the primary endpoint related to the difference from baseline in the Montgomery-Åsberg Depression Rating Scale ((MADRS)) at week six.

The randomized study of 581 patients, running alongside its sister study COMP005, was designed to evaluate the effect of two fixed doses of COMP360 in those with treatment-resistant depression.

Detailed results indicated that in Part A of the study, designed to test two doses of COMP360 administered three weeks apart, reduced the symptom severity, leading to a -3.8-point statistically significant difference in MADRS at the primary endpoint.

Regarding safety, the company stated that the psilocybin-based therapy was generally well-tolerated, and its treatment-emergent adverse events were mild or moderate in severity.

Compass (CMPS) has requested a meeting with the FDA to discuss the results and pursue a rolling submission for a market application, expected to be filed in Q4.

data.title: Rockwell Automation highlights cost savings at Canadian food supplier using Fiix system
data.description:
• Rockwell Automation (ROK) said on Tuesday that Perth County Ingredients, a Canadian supplier of dried egg products, reduced maintenance costs and downtime after implementing its Fiix computerized maintenance management system.
  • The company said Perth County Ingredients achieved a 54% reduction in reactive maintenance and a 47% decrease in after-hours maintenance call-ins, generating more than $40,000 in annual maintenance savings and a threefold return on investment.
  • Rockwell said the supplier integrated plant programmable logic controllers (PLCs) with the Fiix system to enable real-time monitoring of equipment and automated work orders based on preset thresholds.
  • Following the implementation, Perth County Ingredients’ parent organization expanded the Fiix system to seven additional facilities, Rockwell said.
  • ROK -1.61% premarket to $387.99.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Prediction markets push further into the sports world
data.description:
Prediction market platforms continue to push into the sports world.

The NBA All-Star weekend in Los Angeles included a technology summit on Friday that included Polymarket (POLYMARKET) CEO Shayne Coplan, Kalshi (KALSHI) CEO Tarek Mansour, MGM Resorts International (MGM) CEO Bill Hornbuckle, FanDuel (FLUT) CEO Amy Howe, and Sportradar (SRAD) CEO Carsten Koerl. The inclusion of the prediction market executives could indicate a friendlier attitude toward the industry than the cold shoulder from the NFL.

The NFL has banned prediction market ads such as Kalshi (KALSHI) and Polymarket (POLYMARKET) from its broadcasts, including the Super Bowl, and added them to its “prohibited categories” alongside tobacco, firearms, and adult content. The league’s gambling policy explicitly covers prediction markets and bars NFL personnel and players from trading football-related contracts on these platforms or other products that mimic sports wagering.

Bank of America analyst Shaun Kelly said the NBA’s inclusion of prediction market companies on a panel alongside traditional online sports betting operators is worth watching following recent prediction market partnership deals by the NHL, MLS, and UFC, as well as Sportradar's (SRAD) data contract with the NBA. Another interesting tidbit is that NBA star Giannis Antetokounmpo made a recent investment in Kalshi (KALSHI) and is a paid endorser. Antetokounmpo's investment is within the NBA's limit on passive investments in betting or prediction-market entities.

data.title: BrainsWay to change in ADS ratio to 1-for-1, effective March 3
data.description:
• BrainsWay Ltd. (BWAY) on Tuesday said its board has approved a change in the ratio of its American Depositary Shares (ADSs) to ordinary shares to a 1-to-1 structure from the current 2-to-1 ratio.
  • The change, effective before trading begins on March 3, will result in each existing ADS holder receiving one additional ADS for each ADS held, effectively a 2-for-1 forward split of the ADSs.
  • The company said its ADSs will continue to trade on the Nasdaq Capital Market under the symbol “BWAY,” while its ordinary shares, which are not affected by the ratio change, will continue to trade on the Tel Aviv Stock Exchange
  • BrainsWay said the trading price of its ADSs is expected to be halved following the ratio change.
  • ADS holders are not required to take any action.
  • BRSYF closed +32.18% at $11.54.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Fiserv stock jumps on report of Jana Partners building stake, pushing for changes
data.description:
Activist investor Jana Partners is said to have built a stake in Fiserv (FISV) (FISV:CA) and is pushing for changes to boost the payment technology company's stock price.

FISV shares, down ~12% year-to-date, were +4.75% Tuesday pre-market to $62.18.

The stock plunged in October last year as the Milwaukee-based fintech revamped its leadership, refreshed its board, slashed its full-year earnings guidance, and launched a new action plan after its Q3 earnings fell short of Wall Street consensus.

The hedge fund has been speaking privately with Fiserv about ways to improve its stock price, people familiar with the matter told The Wall Street Journal.

The investor wants FISV to accelerate its core banking franchise growth and conduct a strategic review to exit non-strategic assets. However, Jana is not expected to push for a separation of the company's payments business from the fintech unit, the people told WSJ.

Jana supports the One Fiserv plan, which aims to win new enterprise clients and carry out longer-term capital planning. Furthermore, the activist investor supports CEO Mike Lyons, according to the people.

Exact size of Jana's stake in the company and details about the possibility of board representation could not be learned, the Tuesday, February 17, report noted.

data.title: Gilat wins $9 million satellite communications contract from Israel defense ministry
data.description:
• Gilat Satellite Networks (GILT) on Tuesday said that its defense division, Gilat Defense, has secured a $9 million contract from Israel’s Ministry of Defense to supply and integrate satellite communication systems and services.
  • The company said the order includes next-generation defense modems that form part of its ground system defense solution, aimed at supporting secure and mission-critical communications.
  • Gilat said the systems are designed to operate in harsh environments and support a range of operational scenarios.
  • GILT +0.36% premarket to $13.82.
  • Source: Press Release
